proved: Semantic heading, paragraph, quotation, and stable policy blocks from one exact live editor snapshot map into the immutable version schema.
A mismatched expected document revision fails before hashing and inserts no version.
The live editor revision is re-read after asynchronous hashing inside the final version-head transaction; a simulated mid-hash change fails before version/head writes.
The same transaction rechecks the exact expected immutable head and parent bytes before inserting the content-addressed record and moving the head.
The first checkpoint returns no diff note; later checkpoints derive a deterministic engine-free change note from the verified parent.
The Rust MCP advertises and routes save_active_policy_version with required document revision and attribution, optional head/note, and no restore or document-edit semantics.
The compiled debug executable advertises 14 clean stdio tools; the packaged live harness now asserts one-record insertion, head identity, document-revision binding, and post-save integrity.
notProved: The packaged GUI and MCP completed the new checkpoint mutation against a real user profile.
Participant ID/display name is authenticated, enrolled, or portable across installations.
A visible version rail, save button, label editor, restore flow, or branch reconciliation UI.
MCP can restore, delete, rewrite, or directly edit immutable versions; those authorities are intentionally absent.
Drive/WebSocket-backed project collaboration or a simultaneous real second-client edit during checkpoint hashing.
Semantic correctness of the policy or usefulness of the deterministic change note.
